visa_policy.notes: E-visa categories: K2 (tourist, 30 days), K4 (cultural/sports competitions, 30 days), K6 (transit, 10 days); processing time 3 working days via evisa.mn. E-visa is non-extendable and non-convertible. The 34-country temporary visa-free tourism exemption (32 European/EFTA + UK + Australia + NZ) was extended until end of 2026 by government decision of December 31, 2025. Ukraine is NOT listed in visa_free because entry requires an official invitation (conditional grant), and is recorded in conditional_access. South Korea ordinary passport 90-day tourism waiver runs 2022.06.01–2026.12.31 per consul.mn.

cbi.notes: Mongolia does not operate a citizenship-by-investment program. Citizenship through naturalization requires 7 years of lawful residence, Mongolian language ability, knowledge of traditions and constitution, and renunciation of prior citizenship. No official CBI program found on any official Mongolian government domain.

programs.name: Investor Residence Permit (T Visa / Investment Visa)
programs.notes: Foreigners must invest at least USD 100,000 to establish a business entity with foreign investment, comprising at least 25% of business equity. Residency permit is issued for up to 1 year and renewable for up to 3 years. After 5 consecutive years of qualifying lawful residence (minimum 6 months per year), applicant may seek permanent residency. Permanent residency requires legal income of at least 10x minimum wage, Mongolian language ability, knowledge of traditions/customs and Constitution. Processing: ~10 business days via eimmigration.mn portal.

programs.name: K7 Work and Holiday Visa (for Australian nationals)
programs.eligibility: Australian citizens only; issued electronically via e-visa system
programs.notes: Special work and holiday visa category (K7) available electronically to Australian nationals under bilateral arrangement, as part of Mongolia's e-visa expansion per Government Resolution No. 107 of 2024.

visa_free.name: Hong Kong SAR
visa_free.notes: effective 1998.06.18; special administrative region of China

visa_free.name: Macau SAR
visa_free.notes: effective 2004.07.03; special administrative region of China

eta.name: Kenya
eta.notes: visa-free with mandatory eTA registration via etakenya.go.ke; must be filed 14+ days before travel

outbound_official.notes: Source is en.consul.mn/visa/c/84 - the Mongolian Consular Services official site (Ministry of Foreign Affairs domain) listing countries where Mongolian ordinary passport holders are exempted from visa. Page date shown as 2024-09-01. UK, Canada, Japan, Switzerland are listed under special multiple-entry visa arrangements (not visa-free), and are not included in the visa_free list. China ordinary passport holders require an e-visa to enter Mongolia (diplomatic/official only are visa-free on the inbound side); the outbound entry for China in visa_free above refers to Mongolian citizens entering China.

visa_types.name: K2 Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term leisure and tourism travel to Mongolia. Available as an e-visa via evisa.mn.
visa_types.notes: E-visa is non-extendable and non-convertible. Passport must be valid for at least 180 days. Multiple-entry temporary tourist visa (G category) also available for long-term tourists with 30-day max stay per entry and 183–365 day validity.

visa_types.name: G Tourist Visa (Long-term/Multiple Entry)
visa_types.purpose: Long-stay or multiple-entry tourism for foreign nationals who intend to visit Mongolia repeatedly or for extended tourism purposes.
visa_types.notes: Multiple-entry temporary visa. Stay per entry is up to 30 days; validity ranges 183–365 days. Applied at Mongolian embassy/consulate.

visa_types.name: K6 Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through Mongolia en route to a third country. Available as an e-visa.
visa_types.notes: E-visa available via evisa.mn. Non-extendable and non-convertible.

visa_types.name: K3 Border Region Day Pass
visa_types.purpose: For tourists making brief cross-border day trips to Mongolian border regions.
visa_types.notes: Border region tourism only; issued by border immigration authority.

visa_types.name: K1 Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: Short-term business travel including meetings, negotiations, conferences, and commercial activities. Available without an inviting entity for 10-day stays.
visa_types.notes: Single or double entry up to 90 days; validity 150 days. A 10-day business visa is available without an inviting entity per immigration.gov.mn.

visa_types.name: B2 Business/Company Representative Visa
visa_types.purpose: For company representatives, executives, and senior managers of foreign entities operating in Mongolia.
visa_types.notes: B2-1 variant available for accompanying family members. Multiple-entry; validity up to 365 days.

visa_types.name: B1 Investor Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign investors establishing or operating business entities with foreign investment in Mongolia.
visa_types.notes: B1-1 variant for family members. Minimum investment threshold for residence permit pathway is USD 100,000 (25% equity). Visa renewable; residence permit pathway available after qualifying investment.

visa_types.name: B3 Foreign Legal Entity Branch Manager Visa
visa_types.purpose: For managers of registered branch offices of foreign legal entities operating in Mongolia.

visa_types.name: C Visa (Employment Visa - Multiple Sectors)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals employed in Mongolia across various sectors including construction, science, mining, finance, culture, production, agriculture, healthcare, humanitarian services, and transportation.
visa_types.notes: 11 sub-categories (C1–C11) covering different employment sectors. Each sub-category has a family member variant (e.g., C1-1). Requires valid employment contract and sponsoring employer.

visa_types.name: E Visa (Education/Student Visa - Multiple Types)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals studying, conducting research, participating in language/culture/vocational training, or doing internships at Mongolian institutions.
visa_types.notes: Five sub-categories: E1 (general education), E2 (higher education), E3 (language/culture/vocational), E4 (research/internship at state institutions), E5 (innovation and research). E2-1 and E5-1 variants for family members. Requires acceptance letter from accredited Mongolian institution.

visa_types.name: F Visa (Family/Personal Connection Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For spouses of Mongolian citizens, former Mongolian nationals, and their close relatives seeking to reside in or visit Mongolia based on family ties.
visa_types.notes: Three sub-categories: F1 (spouses of Mongolian citizens), F2 (former Mongolian nationals), F3 (close relatives of former nationals). Requires supporting documentation of family relationship.

visa_types.name: H Visa (Personal Purpose Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For distinguished professionals, scholars, and persons with a recognized personal connection to Mongolia, including dual citizenship holders and others with personal purposes.
visa_types.notes: Three sub-categories: H1 (distinguished professionals/scholars), H2 (dual citizenship holders), H3 (other personal purposes).

visa_types.name: J Religious Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals travelling to Mongolia for religious purposes, including missionaries, clergy, and religious organization representatives.
visa_types.notes: Requires documentation from recognized religious organization.

visa_types.name: K4 Cultural and Sports Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals participating in cultural events, arts competitions, or sports competitions held in Mongolia. Available as an e-visa.
visa_types.notes: E-visa available via evisa.mn. Non-extendable and non-convertible. Requires official invitation from organizing body.

visa_types.name: K5 Cargo/Passenger Transport Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals engaged in international cargo or passenger transport operations passing through or operating within Mongolia.
visa_types.notes: For transport crew and operators in international cargo or passenger transit.

visa_types.name: K8 Medical Treatment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals travelling to Mongolia specifically for medical treatment at Mongolian healthcare facilities.
visa_types.notes: Requires confirmation or invitation from a Mongolian medical institution.

visa_types.name: K7 Work and Holiday Visa
visa_types.purpose: Allows eligible Australian nationals to work and travel in Mongolia under a bilateral working holiday arrangement.
visa_types.notes: Available only to Australian citizens. Applicant must be at least 18 and under 31 years of age at time of application. Cannot have previously participated in the program. Applied via the e-visa portal (evisa.mn). Introduced under Government Resolution No. 107 of 2024.

visa_types.name: A1 Official/Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign government officials travelling to Mongolia on official state business, requiring an invitation from Mongolia's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
visa_types.notes: Requires official invitation from Mongolia's Ministry of Foreign Affairs. A1-1 variant for family members. A2 sub-category for accredited foreign media and international news organization staff, with A2-1 for their family members.

validation_notes: CORRECTION 1: Ukraine removed from visa_free and moved to conditional_access (basis=passport_type, ordinary) because consul.mn explicitly states entry requires an official invitation - this is a conditional grant, not an unconditional nationality-based waiver.

CORRECTION 2: All e_visa entries updated from max_stay_days=null to max_stay_days=30 (K2 tourist and K4 cultural/sports), with notes clarifying K6 transit is 10 days, based on immigration.gov.mn and official search results.
CORRECTION 3: South Korea source_url updated from the mfa.gov.mn URL (which returned HTTP 404 on re-fetch) to en.consul.mn/visa/c/83 which confirmed 90-day tourism-only waiver through 2026.12.31.
CORRECTION 4: Agreement dates added to notes for all bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free entries (sourced from live consul.mn page).
VERIFIED: 34-country temporary tourism waiver (32 Schengen-area/EFTA/Monaco/UK + Australia + NZ) is confirmed active through end of 2026 per immigration.gov.mn/mn/articles/536/.
VERIFIED: E-visa country list (97 countries) matches the live immigration.gov.mn/en/evisa-country-list/ page exactly.
VERIFIED: CBI=false, RBI (USD 100,000 investment), and K7 fast-track for Australians remain unchanged and confirmed on official sources.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Full re-fetch of immigration.gov.mn/en/evisa-country-list/ (live page) confirms 97-country e-visa list unchanged - no additions or removals detected. immigration.gov.mn/en/articles/264/ references '99 countries' but the live evisa-country-list page still returns 97; the discrepancy is likely because article/264 was an older announcement that co-mingled Cuba and Paraguay (which have bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free access) into the count. No correction made to e_visa list.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: consul.mn/visa/c/83 re-fetched live - bilateral ordinary-passport exemption list is unchanged from prior run. Most recent addition remains Colombia effective 2026.01.26 (90 days). No new ordinary-passport visa-free countries detected.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: 34-country tourism waiver re-confirmed via immigration.gov.mn/mn/articles/536/ and immigration.gov.mn/en/articles/537/ - still 34 countries, 30-day tourism, through end of 2026 (= until 2027-01-01). List composition unchanged.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Serbia conditional_access entry (diplomatic/official, 90 days, effective 2026.01.30) confirmed present on live consul.mn/visa/c/83 page. Note: Serbia separately cancelled its own inbound visa exemption for Mongolian passport holders (outbound context), which is unrelated to inbound Serbia diplomatic-passport holders entering Mongolia.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-27: Tajikistan does NOT appear in Mongolia's inbound ordinary-passport visa-free list on consul.mn/visa/c/83; it only appears in the outbound (Mongolian citizens visiting Tajikistan) list. No inbound Tajikistan visa-free entry added.
NO CORRECTIONS MADE 2026-06-27: All visa_free, e_visa, conditional_access, cbi, rbi, and fast_track data confirmed accurate against live official pages. File unchanged except this validation note.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: Full re-fetch of en.consul.mn/visa/c/83 confirms 31-country ordinary-passport visa-free list unchanged. Most recent entry remains Colombia (90 days, effective 2026.01.26). No new ordinary-passport additions detected in 2025-2026 beyond what is already recorded. Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, and Oman new agreements (2025-2026) are DIPLOMATIC/SPECIAL passport only, correctly placed in conditional_access not visa_free.
RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: 34-country tourism waiver (30-day tourism, through end of 2026) re-confirmed via immigration.gov.mn/mn/articles/536/ and /en/articles/537/. List composition unchanged.

RE-VERIFIED 2026-06-29: E-visa country list re-fetched from immigration.gov.mn/en/evisa-country-list/ — confirmed 97 countries, unchanged. Note: immigration.gov.mn still shows Colombia in its e-visa list despite the bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free agreement effective 2026.01.26. Our JSON correctly excludes Colombia from e_visa (since bilateral visa-free supersedes e-visa eligibility) and places Colombia only in visa_free, sourced from the more current consul.mn policy page.

NO CORRECTIONS MADE 2026-06-29: All visa_free (31 bilateral + 34 temporary tourism), e_visa (97 countries), conditional_access, cbi, rbi, and fast_track data confirmed accurate against live official pages. File unchanged except this validation note.
gaps: Exact maximum stay for K6 transit e-visa (10 days) comes from web search synthesis, not a verbatim official page text - immigration.gov.mn/en/evisa-country-list/ does not display duration details per-category inline.

Ukraine ordinary-passport rule (requires official invitation, up to 90 days) is based on a Soviet-era 1979 agreement listed on consul.mn; the underlying treaty text and whether it remains in force after 2022 has not been independently verified from an MFA primary source.
No official Mongolian government source found for any credential-based conditional entry rule (e.g., entry granted because traveller holds a valid US/Schengen/EU/UK visa or residence permit).
No official source found for OCI (Overseas Citizenship of India) or similar diaspora/heritage special-status rules granting entry to Mongolia.
investmongolia.gov.mn/visa-information/ remains unverified (socket error in prior session); investor visa details sourced from immigration.gov.mn only.
mfa.gov.mn page for South Korea tourism exemption now returns HTTP 404; underlying data confirmed via consul.mn instead.

immigration.gov.mn/en/articles/264/ cites '99 countries' eligible for e-visa, but the live evisa-country-list page returned 97 on 2026-06-29 re-fetch. The 2-country discrepancy is not explained on the official page - possible that the article was written anticipating additions (Cuba, Paraguay) that have not yet been applied to the live list, or the article count included countries with bilateral visa-free access. Flagged for future monitoring.

Colombia dual-listing gap: immigration.gov.mn still shows Colombia in its e-visa eligible list (as of 2026-06-29 re-fetch) despite bilateral ordinary-passport visa-free agreement effective 2026.01.26. The official government website has not removed Colombia from the e-visa page. Our JSON reflects the policy as confirmed by consul.mn (visa_free), not the stale e-visa list page. This discrepancy should be monitored for resolution.
